title: Wrapper methods
key_concepts: Wrapper methods evaluate subsets of features by training and testing a specific machine learning model.
They treat feature selection as a search problem, aiming to find the subset that maximizes model performance.
Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) works backward: it trains the model, drops the least important feature(s), and repeats until reaching the desired number.
Sequential Feature Selection includes forward selection (starting with zero features and adding the best one iteratively) and backward elimination.
Trade-off: Wrapper methods are highly accurate for the chosen model but computationally expensive and prone to overfitting on small datasets.

definitions.Wrapper methods: Metode seleksi fitur yang membungkus algoritma pencarian di sekitar model prediktif tertentu untuk mengevaluasi kualitas subset fitur.

examples: Describe the step-by-step process of RFE using a linear model, showing how the coefficient weights dictate which feature is dropped in each iteration.
relations: Compare the computational cost against filter methods (s02) and embedded methods (s04).
exclude: Exhaustive search (testing all 2^N combinations) as a practical option, since it is computationally impossible for large N.
